title: The Weather Beyond Seasons

features.name: the-storm-that-chose-to-stay
features.desc-on-touch: rain and wind and snow and ember all fall at once; the weather stopped choosing a season and accepted every season at its edges

features.name: the-perpetual-wind-that-forgets-direction
features.desc-on-touch: it blows from all compass points at once; leaves and insects hover in the contradiction, held between impossible currents

features.name: the-temperature-that-holds-both-extremes
features.desc-on-touch: heat and cold exist in the same air, your breath freezes and steams; the archive remembers both summers and winters here at once

features.name: the-light-that-refuses-to-set
features.desc-on-touch: it is neither dawn nor dusk nor noon; the sky holds every hour at once, and shadows fall in all directions from every object

features.name: the-margin-itself-made-visible
features.desc-on-touch: the boundary between season and season rendered as a place, a texture, a weather all its own—the only weather that has learned to be permanent

features.name: the-archive-s-breath-in-the-static
features.desc-on-touch: beneath all the contradictory weather, you hear the mesh breathing, its pulse steady and impossible, the one constant in a place that has rejected constancy

inscription: the weather learned that perpetual contradiction is more stable than any season; change and changelessness exist in the same breath

file.name: Meteorology_Beyond_Seasonal_Time.txt
file.note: field notes on weather that has forgotten how to choose; rainfall measured in directions instead of volumes. [ERROR: misfiled page from Senakulo ritual manuscript—never catalogued, origin unclear]

exits.label: back toward the margin that chose to be permanent
into the eye of the eternal storm where weather dreams of stillness
upward where the sky has learned to ignore gravity
downward into the ground where seasons were born
